Container.Name: obsidian-git-backup
Container.Overview:
Syncs an Obsidian vault via obsidian-headless (official Obsidian Sync client) and automatically backs it up to a git repository.
Every time Obsidian Sync delivers changes, they are committed and pushed to your git remote — giving you a private, versioned backup of your vault with full history.
Features: - Continuous sync via obsidian-headless - Filesystem-event-driven git commits (no polling) - Configurable debounce period for large vaults - Automatic SSH key generation on first run - Works with E2E encrypted vaults (decrypted locally before git commit)
This container has no web UI and exposes no ports. Monitor it via container logs. Requires outbound internet access (TCP 443 for Obsidian Sync, TCP 22 for SSH git push).
Requires an Obsidian Sync subscription and a git repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc). If your vault uses E2E encryption, enter your password in the E2EE Password field below.
Run one container per vault. For multiple vaults, install the template once per vault and change the container name (e.g. 'obsidian-git-backup-work', 'obsidian-git-backup-personal') and appdata paths. See the project README for full setup instructions.
Container.Requires: An Obsidian Sync subscription and a git repository with SSH access. Before installing: (1) On your desktop or laptop (not the Unraid server), install Node.js, then run 'npm install -g obsidian-headless' and 'ob login' to obtain your auth token (requires a web browser). (2) If the git-watcher stops committing on a busy server, you may need to raise the inotify watch limit on the Unraid host: echo 524288 > /proc/sys/fs/inotify/max_user_watches (add to Settings > Boot > Go file to persist across reboots). After first start: check container logs for the generated SSH public key and add it to your git server (GitHub/GitLab) before the first push can succeed.
Container.Changes: ### 2026-03-03 - Initial Unraid CA template release - Continuous Obsidian Sync via obsidian-headless - Filesystem-event-driven git commits (no polling) - Automatic SSH key generation - E2E encrypted vault support

Config.Name: Obsidian Auth Token
Config.Description: SENSITIVE: grants read access to ALL your Obsidian Sync vaults — treat like a password. To get it: on your desktop/laptop, install obsidian-headless (npm install -g obsidian-headless), run 'ob login' (opens a browser), then find the token file: on macOS/Linux run 'find ~/.obsidian-headless ~/.config/obsidian-headless -name auth_token 2>/dev/null', on Windows check %USERPROFILE%\.obsidian-headless\auth_token. The file contains the raw token string (not JSON). See README for details.

Config.Name: Vault Name
Config.Description: The name of your Obsidian vault exactly as shown in the Obsidian app (e.g. 'My Notes'). Must match exactly — case-sensitive, including spaces. Run 'ob sync-list-remote' on your desktop to list available vaults. You can also use the vault ID (UUID) — required if multiple vaults share the same name.

Config.Name: Git Remote URL
Config.Description: SSH URL of the git remote to push to. Example: git@github.com:username/vault-backup.git

Config.Name: E2EE Password
Config.Description: Only needed if the vault uses E2E encryption. Important: obsidian-headless decrypts the vault locally before writing files. Your git repository will contain plaintext notes — secure your git remote accordingly. E2EE prevents Obsidian's servers from reading your data, but the local copy (and your git backup) is always unencrypted.

Config.Name: Config Storage
Config.Description: Stores SSH keys and obsidian-headless device state. Must survive restarts. If lost, you'll need to re-add the SSH public key to your git server and restart to re-register with Obsidian Sync.

Config.Name: Vault Storage
Config.Description: Vault data and git working tree. Strongly recommended — without this, the container re-clones the entire vault on every restart (slow, degrades git history). For better I/O, ensure your 'appdata' share uses 'cache-prefer' or 'cache-only' mode (set in Unraid's Shares tab). The default path will then automatically land on the cache drive.

Config.Name: Git Branch
Config.Description: Git branch to commit and push to. The branch must already exist on the remote.

Config.Name: Debounce Seconds
Config.Description: Seconds of filesystem quiet before committing. After detecting changes, the watcher waits this long for more changes before committing. For large vaults (10,000+ notes) or slow connections, consider 60-120.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: Leave at 99 unless you know you need a different UID. Unraid's permission repair tools (New Permissions) expect appdata files owned by UID 99. Clearing this field causes the image default (1000) to take effect, which can cause those tools to produce unexpected results.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Leave at 100 unless you know you need a different GID. Unraid's permission repair tools (New Permissions) expect appdata files owned by GID 100. Clearing this field causes the image default (1000) to take effect, which can cause those tools to produce unexpected results.
